title: Which Token Does an API Validate?
description:
## Picking the Right Token at the API

A **resource server (an API)** receives a request from a client and must decide whether the caller is authorized. **Which token** should the API expect and validate?

options.text: The **access token** — it carries the roles/scopes the API uses to authorize the request, and is sent as a Bearer token.
The **ID token** — it identifies the user, so the API should authorize based on it.
The **refresh token** — the API exchanges it for permissions on each call.
Any of the three; they are interchangeable as long as the signature is valid.

explanation: APIs authorize requests using the **access token**, which carries the scopes and roles and is sent in the `Authorization: Bearer` header. Option **b** is a classic mistake: the **ID token** is intended for **the client** to learn who logged in — it is *not* meant for API authorization, and its audience is the client, not the API. Option **c** is wrong — the **refresh token** is used only against Keycloak's token endpoint to get new access tokens; an API never accepts it. Option **d** is false: the tokens have different audiences and purposes and are **not** interchangeable, even when all signatures verify.

hints.title: Check the audience
hints.body: An access token's `aud` targets the API; an ID token's audience is the client. The audience tells you who's supposed to consume it.
